novelty.9-10: No product in any vertical solves this problem in this way — web search returns zero direct competitors
novelty.7-8: Competitors exist but have fundamentally different approaches or serve different user segments
novelty.5-6: A few direct competitors exist but have clear gaps this idea exploits
novelty.3-4: This idea is a feature of an existing product, not a standalone product
novelty.1-2: Thin wrapper over an existing product (GPT wrapper, Stripe dashboard clone)
novelty.0: This is an existing product described differently

usefulness.9-10: Eliminates a painful manual process that affects many developers/teams daily
usefulness.7-8: Meaningfully speeds up a real task; users would pay for it
usefulness.5-6: Nice-to-have; users would use it if free but probably not pay
usefulness.1-2: Solves a problem most people don't have

feasibility.9-10: Core built with 3-5 standard npm packages; no novel algorithms; works locally
feasibility.7-8: Requires one non-trivial integration (one external API, one ML model) but well-documented
feasibility.5-6: Requires two complex integrations or a non-standard infrastructure component
feasibility.3-4: Requires specialized knowledge or > 6 months of development
feasibility.1-2: Requires team of 3+ or research-level ML

testability.9-10: Single binary metric: task completion rate, latency, error rate
testability.5-6: Success is measurable but requires qualitative user feedback alongside metrics
testability.3-4: Success is hard to define; fuzzy outcomes
testability.1-2: Long feedback loops (months to know if it worked)

speedToMvp.9-10: Scriptable CLI or single-page demo in < 1 week
speedToMvp.5-6: 1-2 month MVP with a real user testing it
speedToMvp.1-2: 6+ months before any real user feedback
speedToMvp.0: No path to an MVP that validates the core hypothesis

defensibility.9-10: Network effects, proprietary data, or a moat that requires years to build
defensibility.7-8: Developer workflow lock-in, integration network, or unique domain expertise
defensibility.5-6: First-mover advantage + community, but copyable with enough effort
defensibility.3-4: Commodity implementation; any developer could clone it
defensibility.1-2: Already being built by a funded competitor
defensibility.0: Exact copy of an existing product

clarity.9-10: One sentence describes who has the problem, what the problem is, and why now
clarity.7-8: Problem is clear; target user slightly generic
clarity.5-6: Problem is understandable but solution is vague
clarity.3-4: Problem is a symptom, not a root cause
clarity.1-2: Could mean multiple different things to different people
